name: Upstart
description_short: Upstart offers personal loans ($1,000-$75,000), car refinancing, HELOCs, and short-term cash relief using AI-powered underwriting. They claim next-day funding and rates 33% lower than traditional lenders.
description_long:
Upstart is a fintech lending platform that has served over 4 million customers. Founded on AI-powered underwriting principles, the company leverages alternative data and machine learning to assess creditworthiness beyond traditional credit scores. Their platform originated as a personal loan provider but has expanded into multiple consumer lending products over time.

Upstart's primary offerings include unsecured personal loans ranging from $1,000 to $75,000 with fixed rates between 6.2% and 35.99% APR, car loan refinancing with APRs from 5.17% to 29.99%, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs) offered through Upstart Home Lending ranging from $26,000 to $250,000, and a short-term cash relief product (Cash Line) from $200 to $2,500 with APRs up to 36%. All personal loan products include no prepayment penalties and claim next-day funding (99% within 1 business day after signing).

Upstart distinguishes itself through several operational features: rate checks use soft credit inquiries that don't impact credit scores, the application process takes approximately 5 minutes, and approval decisions are provided instantly. They advertise that personal loan APRs are 33% lower than traditional models, and car refinance customers average $126 in monthly savings. The company emphasizes transparent pricing with no hidden fees and offers a broad approval range including applicants across varying income levels.

While Upstart's AI underwriting model and fast funding are genuine advantages, the company's highest APR rates (35.99% for personal loans, 36% for Cash Line) approach or meet predatory lending thresholds. The short-term cash product, despite being framed as "relief," carries costs comparable to payday loans. Additionally, certain credit unions may place liens on customer accounts, and the company offers the option to secure personal loans with vehicles—features that complicate the "unsecured" marketing claim. Rates and approval depend heavily on individual credit profiles, and advertised savings figures are based on specific cohorts rather than guaranteed outcomes.

Borrowers comparing personal loan lenders should consider the full range of borrowing and credit-building options available. Those with damaged credit may find personal loans for bad credit more accessible, though typically at higher rates. Debt consolidation loans are specifically designed to combine multiple high-interest balances into a single payment with a lower rate. For credit rebuilding alongside borrowing, credit builder loans and secured credit cards offer structured paths to improving scores over time. Consumers dealing with existing negative items should also explore credit repair services to address inaccuracies before applying, as a cleaner credit report often unlocks better loan terms. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 2950 S Delaware St Suite 300, San Mateo, CA 94403

tiers.name: Personal Loans
features: Soft credit check for rate preview

tiers.name: Auto Refinancing
features: Not available in IA, LA, MD, NV, PR

pros: Next-day funding for 99% of approved personal loans after signing
Rate checks use soft inquiries that don't impact credit score
No prepayment penalties on personal loans—pay off early without fees
Fixed-rate personal loans eliminate payment uncertainty
Instant approval decisions after application submission
Broad approval criteria across varying income levels
No hidden fees and transparent pricing structure
Offers multiple loan products (personal, auto refinance, HELOC, short-term relief)
cons: Personal loan APRs reach 35.99% and short-term cash APRs reach 36%—comparable to high-cost lending
Advertised rate advantages (33% lower) apply only to qualified applicants; lowest rates unavailable to most
Some credit unions may place liens on customer accounts despite 'unsecured' marketing
Short-term cash product ($200-$2,500) functions similarly to payday loans with high APRs despite 'relief' framing
Approval and rate quality heavily dependent on credit profile, income, and undisclosed factors
best_for: Borrowers with fair-to-good credit seeking debt consolidation with fixed rates and no prepayment penalties
Car owners with existing auto loans looking to refinance and reduce monthly payments
Homeowners with equity seeking larger credit lines up to $250,000
Consumers who prefer transparent, fast online lending processes without traditional bank friction
diagnosis: Upstart is the leading AI-powered personal lending platform, using 1,600+ variables beyond traditional credit scores to evaluate borrowers. This makes it a strong option for borrowers with thin credit files, recent graduates, or people whose creditworthiness isn't captured by FICO alone — Upstart claims to approve 101% more borrowers at 38% lower rates than traditional models. The tradeoff: origination fees can reach 12% (among the highest in the industry), APRs top out at 35.99%, and the SEC subpoenaed Upstart in November 2023 regarding AI model disclosures (outcome unclear as of 2026). With ~1,400 employees, NASDAQ listing (UPST), and 100+ bank/credit union partners, Upstart has institutional scale but borrower experience varies significantly by credit tier.
services: Personal loans ($1,000-$75,000) with fixed APR rates 6.2%-35.99%
Car loan refinancing with APR rates 5.17%-29.99%
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) $26,000-$250,000 at rates from 6.52% APR
Cash Line short-term relief ($200-$2,500) payable over 3-18 months
Soft credit inquiry rate checks (no impact to credit score)
Optional vehicle-secured personal loans with lien placement

contact.address: 2950 S Delaware St Suite 300, San Mateo, CA 94403
